title: [CI:DOCS] Just restore protections of shadow-utils
body:
Base images don't have shadow-utils permissions set correctly, this
change should speed up the building of images a little bit.

[NO TESTS NEEDED] This does not change buildah in any way, so no need to
tests.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

For me, this change broke the file capabilities of `/bin/newuidmap`.
The reinstall of `shadow-utils` is necessary for me to get the correct file capabilities:
```
$ podman run --rm -it --pull always quay.io/buildah/stable
[root@676c0a7ca1b6 /]# getcap /bin/newuidmap
[root@676c0a7ca1b6 /]# rpm --restore --quiet shadow-utils
[root@676c0a7ca1b6 /]# getcap /bin/newuidmap
[root@676c0a7ca1b6 /]# yum -y -q reinstall shadow-utils                                                   
[root@676c0a7ca1b6 /]# getcap /bin/newuidmap
/bin/newuidmap cap_setuid=ep
```

Yikes, looks like changing just the xattrs wasn't enough for the tooling that's building the images to realize that it needed to include them in the layer for that step.  That's a surprise, but it implies that we need to revert this one.

What do you think is going on?  It seems like the quiet is causing the issue.

Could you confirm that this works without the --quiet

```
$ podman run --privileged --rm -it --pull always quay.io/buildah/stable
Trying to pull quay.io/buildah/stable:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 9c4a2113588b skipped: already exists  
Copying blob ca594071dfff skipped: already exists  
Copying blob a58e173d4901 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 5c5f06f916d7 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob a2a5a94b8e24 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying blob 933fc4260fc8 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config b88d4336fd done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
[root@6d22bc5cf179 /]# rpm -qV shadow-utils
........P    /usr/bin/newgidmap
........P    /usr/bin/newuidmap
[root@6d22bc5cf179 /]# rpm --quet --restore shadow-utils
rpm: --quet: unknown option
[root@6d22bc5cf179 /]# rpm --quiet --restore shadow-utils
[root@6d22bc5cf179 /]# rpm -qV shadow-utils
........P    /usr/bin/newgidmap
........P    /usr/bin/newuidmap
[root@6d22bc5cf179 /]# rpm --restore shadow-utils 2> /dev/null
[root@6d22bc5cf179 /]# rpm -qV shadow-utils
```

> What do you think is going on? It seems like the quiet is causing the issue.

Wow, I wouldn't have guessed that was it, but it makes sense that `--quiet` would interfere with the expansion of `--setcaps`.  That's quite a relief.
